reviews.title: Everything and more from what i expected from a smart watch
reviews.text: i spent a great deal of time researching smart watches, Garmin came up the winner.  I have dealt with their customer service on two occasions with another product and found them to be excellent which i a big reason for buying another Garmin product.

reviews.title: Poor battery life, feature rich
reviews.text:
If only the battery life was better... I have the GPS turned off and I only measure my blood oxygen level when I'm asleep yet it is nearly impossible for the watch to last a little more than 24 hours.

My next Garmin will be the one with a solar powered assist.

That being said for the negatives this watch has the features I wanted at a good price:

- Sleep quality/quantity
- Blood oxygen level
- Steps counter
- Flight of stairs counting
- Water intake recording ) manual input)
- Multiple activities recording (it can detect when I'm running, bike riding, etc.)

It has been durable and the screen is still scratch free.

If poor battery life isn't a deal breaker buy it.

reviews.title: After a year and a half...can't wait to switch, Spotify is the worst part of this watch by far
reviews.text:
I have owned this watch for a year and a half now. I use it almost daily for my walks/runs, weight workouts, and indoor bike workouts. The main reason I chose this watch in the first place was because it was one of the smaller ones available. I am 5'9 but have small wrists, and really disliked the 'utilitarian' look of larger watches. After a year and a half, I am so tired of the issues with it and ready to jump ship. If I used this watch more regularly (I only wear it for workouts - not all day), I would have switched a LONG time ago. The amount of people who have complained about the same issues for YEARS without Garmin doing anything makes me hesitant to purchase another Garmin product, especially with the Apple Watch, Samsung, and now Google buying out Fitbit and coming up with a watch.
- Spotify on this watch seriously sucks. If you check Spotify forums, you will find COUNTLESS threads of suggestions for the Spotify app on this watch that should truly be basic features at this price. Do they implement them or listen? No. Also, there is no way to contact Spotify customer service - you just get pointed towards their 'community forum' where you have people complaining of the same exact things with no fix. You can't skip forward on any track, at all. I listen to a lot of podcasts when I walk. Some are 2 hours in length while my walk is only 1 hour. This watch constantly either reverts to the very beginning of my podcast so I have to start all over (because again, you can't skip forward - so have fun listening to ads as well!) in anything, only updates 1 song out of 15 on my playlist or get the error 'sync failed', or some other random issue. Also, if you have a playlist on Spotify, you cannot choose the track you want to listen to. You have to hit the >> button to get to where you want. Really? I hope Garmin drops Spotify because they have been a truly horrible app on this watch the entire time with no effort to make updates. Take a look at the reviews of Spotify on the Garmin store if you don't believe me.
- Battery life is a couple of days. However, there are points in time where my watch will be at say 80% the night before, I get ready to leave and go my walk the next morning, pick up my watch, and it's at 8%. WTF? This happens enough to be super annoying and apparently is not an 'only me' problem. It appears to be tied to when the watch updates.
- I tried to wear this to track my sleep in the beginning and gave up after two nights due to the extreme disco effect of the 'gesture' motions. I tried turning on the 'do not disturb' function advertised on the watch which is supposed to disable this stupidly bright light that WILL wake you and whoever is next to you up. Didn't work. Do a search for 'Garmin watch lights up in middle of the night' and you will find all sorts of people complaining of the same thing, and not just on this version of the watch.
- GPS has issues connecting and can take several minutes which is pretty annoying when you try to start tracking an activity only to see a red bar when trying to connect. I 'sync' my watch to my phone constantly so that is not the issue. I have switched between the GPS options Garmin offers on the watch to no avail. Search for this issue as well and you will see lots of people with the same complaint.

reviews.title: GET THIS WATCH!!!
reviews.text: I LOVE this watch!!! It is so easy to use, tracks all of the metrics I am interested in (steps, sleep, stress, activities, hydration, menstrual cycle) and then some!! It is very accurate and the app is very user- friendly. The watch itself has worked perfectly and I have found it to be comfortable and intuitive to use.  It holds a charge for several days on end and when it does need to be charged, it reaches full battery very quickly! I looked at a lot of different watches and considered a lot of different options. I am so happy that I went with the vivoactive 4! Garmin, you all did a great job with this watch!! I honestly could not be happier with my choice!!

reviews.title: A Gift that went well
reviews.text:
This was a Christmas gift for my wife to replace her older Vivosmart. She wanted the new features but she has a small wrist.
Doing some searches I thought this would be as big as I should try.

All went well. It does all she needs and while it's much better it's not too big. Doing larger does add more battery time and more features, but this is the right fit for her

reviews.title: doesn't stay on securely
reviews.text: I've purchased one of these in the past and liked it, but the gold color faded so I l reordered another it in black. However, this time, one side kept disconnecting and the attachment site looked defective compared to the other side causing me to almost lose my Fitbit twice. Luckily, I was indoors and heard it drop. I will return it and try a different brand as I fear I will encounter this issue again if I simply replace it.  This style is nice because it's easy to put on and off even with a single hand as opposed to the type of bracelet that comes with the Fitbit.

reviews.title: Worried the magnet will damage device
reviews.text: I wanted a band that would have more variability in fit, and I liked the color, so I ordered this.  It is, indeed, compatible with the Fitbit Sense and I enjoyed it for several days until I had to charge my Sense.  I then discovered that the magnet was so strong that I had difficulty keeping it off the back of my device long enough to get the charger on.  I was worried that strong magnet might damage the circuits and contacted FitBit for their opinion.  They weren't sure, but pointed out that they do have a hook-and-loop wristband in-house that is available.  Since I was also having trouble with the edges of the VNCAS band getting caught on my wool shawl and anything else fuzzy I wore, I decided to ditch it for the FitBit brand option.  Sorry, but I can't recommend this one.

reviews.title: Not Well Made😳
reviews.text: Initially when I bought this watch band, I thought it was nice, solid and seemed to be well made. I've been wearing it just a little over 2 months now and there's some sharp little burrs that have now showed up on the watch band itself and the edges of the other parts are now seeming to be sharp as well and keep getting hung up on my clothing and have also scratched my boyfriend several times. The finish is wearing off on the backside of the magnet connector area so now it looks very tarnished. I do wear it everyday all day long, but I don't feel after such a short period of time it should look so bad and have these sharp edges now. Unfortunately it's past my return window so I'm going to have to just throw it away and get another one. Such a shame because I really like the magnetic band and the color. I'm a little bit leery about buying another similar style for fear that the same thing will happen again. 😳

reviews.title: Cute but it snags
reviews.text: It snags on everything. Ugh. So I can't wear it with nice blouses or sweaters it gets caught and causes holes, runs, etc. Otherwise I love it.

reviews.title: Like the fact that it's magnetic
reviews.text: Be careful since it's magnetic it may stick to your silverware or metal objects

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

reviews.title: Comfortable, worth the value
reviews.text: The band that originally came with the Fitbit was so uncomfortable. It dig into my wrist and left marks. I ordered another band that was about the same price as all four of these. It was useless. Barely fit, and it wouldn’t keep its position, so I decided to try these. First, I couldn’t believe that the same price got me FOUR bands in different colors! The bands are easily adjustable. Once fitted, these stay in place on the wrist. They’re comfy, and they stretch enough so that I can pull off the Fitbit to charge without having to readjust the band. Though inexpensive, this particular purchase was one of the best I’ve had this year, LOL.

reviews.title: Really love them but 2 already broke after 2 weeks
reviews.text: I really love these, I hate the bands that come with the fitbits. These are really comfortable but 1 band wouldn't work for some reason then another band broke after 2 weeks without even consistently wearing it.

reviews.title: Did NOT Work with Sense 2
reviews.text: Not sure what the issue is, but I could not get any of these bands to engage and lock with the Sense 2.  The tab fits into the slot just fine, but it won't engage the locking tab to hold the band into place.  Pulling on the band at all causes it to simply fall out of the watch case.  The bands themselves look fine, but not locking into place obviously makes them worthless for their intended purpose.

reviews.title: Don't Bother. I have tried 4 of these from them for my Charge 5. Also comes loose from the Fitbit
reviews.text: Headline says it all. My first order, I was hoping it was just a rando, but exchanged, and every single one has the same problem.  Just sloppy construction -- enough out of spec that it might or might not work. I love the band. It's a crying shame it doesn't stay connected to the fitbit. Can't imagine how there could be even a single 5-star -- maybe the first batches were fabricated correctly.  Unfortunately there's nothing as nice or comfortable that I've been able to find for charge-5, so I may end up getting yet a 5th and 6th trial and frankensteining them with parts that do work to get something that works and something I like. I'll let you know

reviews.title: works to add security to fitbit watch band
reviews.text:
Good. I have been using these bands as extra security on my fitbit bands since I first got a fitbit dating back to 2015. I have had several styles since then, and I always make sure to have a pack of these security bands on hand. First it was because the fitbit band was just a pressure close band that easily popped off, then it was a matter of keeping a regular watch band style extra secure. These work on the regular width bands.

When I was ready to buy more of these security bands, I was about to buy some that are made for fitbit, but then I realized that those have a protrusion for locking into the band. I really just wanted something to slip on with no need to make use of the band holes to secure it. These are exactly what I wanted. Nice variety of colors.

reviews.title: Amazing Feature Packed Smart Watch at a Spectacular Cost
reviews.text:
This VALYV K22 Military Sport Watch is packed with an amazing number of smart features and provided seamless integration with my Samsung smart phone. The display is crystal-clear, and the audio capability and volume are very impressive. In general, it’s a very handsome watch and has a very intuitive user interface. The price is unbelievably low in comparison to some of the other smart watches on the market.
I especially enjoy the built-in fitness, activity, and sports tracking features which have already proven to be invaluable while training at the gym as well as general health monitoring during my activity throughout the day.
This watch charges quickly using the included USB magnetically connected cable and the battery is long lasting once charged. The user interface is customizable through the app – I preferred the default one that it shipped with… but there are MANY other options to choose from.
I summary, after spending many years not even wearing a watch… I am very happy with this purchase and can definitely recommend the purchase of this useful product to others.

reviews.title: Really great for the price!
reviews.text: I don’t spend a lot of money on things like this so I didn’t know what to expect since it’s not $300+… it’s not bad for the price! It works well, the stats seem fairly accurate, except the blood pressure which is always showing ridiculously good (I wish)!! I have high blood pressure and this watch has me at 117/ 68 most of the time. Other then that the only problem I have is the charge! It has a cable with 2 tiny magnets, you have to line them up just so and as soon as you’ve got it, it pops off!!

reviews.title: DO NOT BUY worked less than 60 days and no customer support.
reviews.text:
DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. YOU WILL REGRET IT!!!!
UPDATE: Less than 60 days and the watched completely stopped working.
Being new to using a smart watch I didn't want to invest a fortune. Read lots of reviews and decided to give this one a try. It is perfect. It does everything I want it to do. It looks great. It is simple to set it up as you want. AND they provided exceptional customer service!

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text: This watch has never worked and the instructions did not help.

reviews.title: There are TWO ways of rating this... Garbage or almost garbage.
reviews.text:
There are two ways of rating purchases... either an overall evaluation IRREGARDLESS of the price OR... CONSIDERING THE PRICE. Considering I paid $20 for it... not horrible. BUT here are the SERIOUS flaws so far:

-The Blood Pressure monitor is a COMPLETE JOKE. "Inaccurate" is NOT the word. I tried it on several friends and coworkers. Garbage!
-You are supposed to be able to make a gesture of bringing your wrist up to view the watch and then it is supposed to turn the screen on so that you can read it. ALMOST NEVER! No matter HOW I make the gesture the only thing that works is a PAINFUL twist/shake of my wrist and then it's not guaranteed. I just use the button instead which kind of defeats the purpose.
-BUT THEN, when sleeping.... EVERY DAMNED MOVEMENT I MAKE it turns on... and waking up my wife, because it is like having a flashlight shone in your face!!!!!!!!!!!

So... EVEN THOUGH this was only $20... SAVE YOUR MONEY FOR SOMETHING THAT ACTUALLY WORKS AND WORKS WELL.

reviews.title: Great Smart Watch!
reviews.text: It is a great dupe of Apple Watch.  I absolutely love it. The greatest attraction is the extended battery time (7 to 10 days). I also like various fitness track features such as step count, heart-beat, workout routine. I could check weather too. Another interesting feature is the multiple watch face options. However I struggled a bit figuring out how to change those watch faces. The watch faces can be changed easily using the APP. I found the questions and answers mentioned on the webpage pretty helpful when figuring out other elements.

reviews.title: Bad watch!

reviews.title: Outstanding value
reviews.text: I got this for my spouse who has an android phone.  He absolutely loves it!  He was so thrilled with it and how easy it was to set up and use that he gave me a list of the pros and cons. Pros: comfortable; stylish (never heard him use that word before lol); there's a watchface for all tastes and occasions; the features are intuitive and the charge lasts for 24+ hours.  The only cons he mentioned was that it did not give an approximate charge time and that he can't figure out how to add contacts. I've never seen him so enthusiastic about a fitness watch before and he previously disdained them. Based on his experience, I would highly recommend this one.

reviews.title: It works as a watch but most other functions are suspect
reviews.text:
This smart watch looks nice enough - someone could easily confuse it for an older Apple Watch - and its easy to make custom watch faces (I put a pic I took at an Iron Maiden show on mine). And it is jam packed with features - way more than some other watches in this price range.

Unfortunately, I have trouble trusting its more advanced features.

A number of times when taking my temperature the watch says I have hypothermia, measuring me between 87-95f. I am a 200lb man living in in Southern California and my wife won’t turn the thermostat down below 74 degrees. I don’t have hypothermia. If it can’t properly read my body temperature, there’s no way I can trust its accuracy with many of the other health features - blood oxygen, blood pressure, heart rate, etc

That step count seems pretty accurate but ONLY once I paired the watch with the Da Fit app. Before I paired the watch, it measure me taking like 350 steps while just sitting on my couch. Not sure why it would measure differently paired or unpaired but it did - significantly. I would not recommend this as a fitness tracker for someone without a smart phone (yes, there are still a few people without smart phones).

And the sleep tracker seems to at least get my sleep times accurate, though I am unsure how well it reads light/deep sleep as I don’t really trust the readings from it’s health sensors. I want to believe its doing a good job. I like the way the app presents the sleep timeline and information. But I just don’t know if its accurate because the watch thinks I have hypothermia.

As for the non-health related functions, they are pretty spotty too. I stopped receiving text notifications after a couple days and could not get them to start back up again without completely disconnecting the phone from the app and starting over again.

Pros:
- Looks like an old gen Apple Watch.
- Customizable face
- Lots of features
- Doesn’t technically *need* a smart phone for many features
- Step counter seems accurate when paired
- Great battery life for a smart watch. I wore it for 5 days without charging.
- Nice app

Cons:
- Highly inaccurate step counter when it is not paired
- Health features are unreliable
- Notifications are unreliable
- The speaker in the watch sounds terrible
- Vibration function is weak and easily missable in active situations
- Sometimes the watch doesn’t ‘wake’ from sleep as easily/instantly as it should. I find myself having to give it an extra shake to wake it almost every time.

reviews.title: Great affordable smartwatch
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:52a9c3b58a7816ac48b831c65b3aa6ca]] This is a great smartwatch at a reasonable price. It works really well, making and receiving calls, and all of the other features. It is lightweight and charges really fast. The battery life is really good too. You cannot beat the price. Great value and highly recommended.

reviews.title: Unboxing
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:53bca64d7f3459321ad803eb09c234eb]] Let's start off with what I liked. I liked that is not heavy and feels comfortable. I like the size of the overall screen of the watch. I liked that it is easy to connect to your android device. I do not like that after a free trial service of the texting and monitoring of all the apps, you have to purchase monitoring space or data. I don't like that you are limited to how many contacts you can store on the device. I do not like the overall "ability to text feature." I feel that the text input data can be improved. Down to what and how you reply via messages.

title: Cillso Smart Watch for Men, IP68 Waterproof Fitness Tracker, Smartwatch for Android iPhone Phones, Activity Tracker with Heart Rate and Sleep Monitor, 24 Sports Modes, Pedometer, Black
features: More Personal, Clearer Touch Screen: 1.3 inch tempered HD LCD with IPS technology for enhanced color and clarity. And covered with an aluminum alloy frame, so elegant and light to wear, but sturdy and anti-scratch.The smart watch contains 5 dials readily available to choose from along with another 50 more dials within the app. You can also with custom setting your favorite photo from phone album as screen background to meet your daily needs and define different moods.

Your Personal Smart Assistant: Activity tracker (pedometer, calories, distance), heart rate, various sport modes, sleep monitoring,etc. Get notifications of Call, SMS, and APP messages(The fitness tracker watch can not make messages, calls or text back), remote control of music and cameras, weather report, sedentary reminder, alarm clock, stopwatch, find phone, call rejection, brightness adjustment, power-saving mode, and other functions to meet your daily needs.

Long Battery and IP68 Waterproof: The waterproof smart watch can be used for 10-15 days or 30 days standby time, much longer than most traditional smartwatch, and only takes 2.5 hours to charge. The fitness watch is IP68 waterproof, you can wear it when washing hands, in the rain(Note: Do Not use in water more than 100. 40℉, such as showers, saunas, spring, etc).

24 Sports Modes and Health Monitoring: Our fitness tracker for men women supports 24 sports modes and allows you to record fitness data including steps, distance, calories burned, miles walked. Advanced HR sensor for accurate and effective heart rate monitoring, helping you better understand your health and making a reasonable adjustment to your lifestyle. Automatic sleep tracking can monitor your sleep status and provide a comprehensive sleep quality analysis to help become healthier.

Smart Notifications and Compatibility: The rugged smart watch is compatible with most iOS 9.0 & Android 5.0 or higher smart phones. Pair our activity tracker to your phone and receive all your SNS notifications straight to your round smart watch including Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Whatsapp, and more (Please note: messages are for viewing only). And support control the music playback or camera on your smartphone.

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
Pretty good smartwatch. I needed something to replace my Galaxy Active, but didn't want to spend $100+ on something with a horrible battery life. I bought this one and for a while I was disappointed. I had to charge the watch each night and the steps weren't as accurate. I changed the setting so that the screen doesn't turn on when you move your wrist and that has saved the battery big time. I'm on day 4 and only down to 50% which means I'm now able to track sleep as well. I still think the steps aren't as accurate because it almost matches the steps in my phone's health app, but I'm notorious for leaving my phone places. I also miss being able to limit the options for exercise types in the watch face. I have to scroll through a long list each time. Overall, pretty good product for a cheaper price.

Update: I originally gave this 5 stars, but I have to take 2 away. A few days ago, the watch all of a sudden stopped counting steps. For the price, it was perfect until it stopped working. Back to the drawing board.

reviews.title: The perfect watch
reviews.text: Hubby loves his birthday watch , has no complaints. Says it’s the perfect watch, for all he uses it for. .

title: AMAZTIM Smart Watch, 60 Days Extra-Long Battery, 50M Waterproof, Rugged Military Bluetooth Call(Answer/Dial Calls) Fitness Tracker, 1.85" Ultra Large HD Display, AI Voice Assistant 24H Sleep Monitor
features: ⌚Military-Standard quality - AMAZTIM M2 features 1.85-inch second generation Corning Gorilla screen. As the first 9H twin glass cover, it's strong enough to deal with a range of screen-destroying behaviors. The TC4 titanium unibody and the exclusive developed two-color silicone rubber is the world's first two-color integration in a smart watch; military-grade materials and fully enclosed design makes it the toughest and sturdiest watch. Successfully passed 12 Military Tests Including, extreme temperature,dust resistance, salt spray, fluid pollution,etc. Certified to MIL-STD810H , Our smart watches for men ensures that you use them confidently in a variety of harsh conditions.

⌚50M Waterproof /Extremely resistant to damage - Incredibly strong and corrosion-resistant titanium case has been used to enclose and giving cover, the rugged military grade smart watch with fully enclosed ultra-thin case design can be easily submerged into 50M (IP69K waterproof) deep water. Our durable smartwatch has withstood the extreme conditions and passed drilling, crushing and hot steam washout tests, making our sports watch suitable to use under high temperature and pressure.

⌚IndustryLeading dual smart chip - ① AI Voice Assistant & Make Calls: A very handy tool built in the watch. Use your voice to give instructions and the voice assistant will do the rest. You can ask questions, play your desired music, and check weather, set alarms and timers. You can also make voice calls through the voice assistant, makes the scenes that are inconvenient to hold a mobile phone in daily exercise and life more concise and smart. ② Intelligent sports recognition: features BioTracker PPG optical sensor and the advanced AI algorithm,M2 enables smart recognition of 6 sports. The system can automatically sense current sports status without entering Sports Modes manually.

⌚60+ Days super battery life - M2 is equipped with a 100% pure cobalt battery (military grade), the battery capacity is as high as 380mAh ensuring optimal performance and longevity which is twice as much as that of conventional smart watches. At the same time, super power-saving AI algorithm breaking the industry is adopted to minimize power consumption, enough to last up to 2 weeks under normal use, and can continue to use sports mode, health index measurement and other functions for up to 60+ days in power-saving mode. With 30-minute charge, you can have enough battery backup to last for up to 3 days.

⌚24H Health monitoring /70 Exercise modes - AMAZTIM M2 collects the health data with its advanced real-time monitoring function. The fitness watch helps you monitor All-day sleep, 24-hour heart rate, and real-time blood pressure and blood oxygen. The fitness tracking watch includes a pedometer to keep track of the calories burned throughout the day. Supports up to 70 sports modes including indoor and outdoor exercises such as hiking, skiing, swimming, etc. The smartwatches collects the data with pinpoint precision and accuracy, ensuring that every workout is counted.

reviews.title: Easy to set up, very accurate, keeps me updated with many aspect of my health
reviews.text:
I really appreciate this watch and the features it has. First off, the smart watch was very easy to pair to my phone. I have an Android phone, and it only took a few minutes to pair the watch with my phone.
First enable Bluetooth and find the device listed as "C20"

Next, download the app that's shown in the QR code. Once you have the watch paired via Bluetooth to your smart phone and the app is downloaded, your watch ready to go, and should be syncing to your smart phone. The connection has been great thus far; it hasn't disconnected/lost connection to my phone.

As someone who has suffers from pulmonary lung disease, I was very excited when I found out this smart watch has the capability to track my oxygen saturation, heart rate, and blood pressure. In addition, you don't have to do anything, the watch will automatically to measurements periodically throughout the day.

It's helping me monitor and compare results over a few days. I suggest authorizing Google Fit so your hearth history is accessible when it. For me, it helps to have a history when I meet with my pulmonologist.

The watch has great standby battery life, and it's light weight. After m three days it's only dipped minimally in available battery percentage. I highly recommend this!

**Updated**

So it's been several days now since I first posted the review and I am amazed how little the battery has drained. Needless to say this smartwatch has fantastic standby battery life; especially considering it's constantly looking for/measuring heart rate. As you can see from the photos the readings are very accurate. I own a pulse oximeter and a blood pressure monitor to keep my pulmonary hypertension in check. My resting heart rate is 95 bpm, which may seem high, but the reading is accurate and normal which makes this product more the reassuring.

reviews.title: Great Watch!
reviews.text: Ok, I have had fitness watches from Samsung to Fitbit to Garmin and this one is the best....especially for the price.  First thing since turning 60 I need a watch I can read in the sunlight.  This has a brightness level I actually do not have to turn it all the way up and the workout data is big enough to read. I think all the health data is correct, but do not trust the Blood Pressure readings because of the science behind it. Actually do not trust any BP on a fitness watch.  There is a lot of customization you can do......I recommend a custom watch face along with setting up quick view on a schedule. I am now going to see if I can get a spare charging cord,

reviews.title: Cheap, Inaccurate, but Cheap
reviews.text:
What works, the clock.
What doesn't work, everything else.
Bought in August, really tried to give it a chance. I knew for the price it was a gamble.

reviews.title: Step count
reviews.text:
Like the look, heft, easy set up.

But I question the accuracy since driving 1 miles and it's counted 30 steps, this is AFTER it logged 82 steps during at my desk.

3 month update. Battery life is still excellent however the watch has stopped connecting to the app

6 month update: Batter life is excellent, I notice it has started to decline, but that could also be heavier use. Step count still seems a bit wonky, but not egregiously off. The Strap is starting to break at the clasp and I will need to replace as soon as I'm able to find a replacement.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Having used different smart watches was looking forward to this one. But it disappointed me. Accuracy on everything was off compared to reality. Other smart watches for less were more accurate. I returned said watch.

This band is really excellent quality. Fit is great, leather is great. It is stiff (leather) at first, but that is like any leather product. It quickly adapts to your wrist and looks great. Their customer service is what I expect, top notch.

reviews.title: Looks good for the price and seems sturdy so far.
reviews.text: Looks good. Came flat and needed a few days to soften up. Not the nicest leather but this was a bargain FitBit band, not a Coach handbag. Very pleased.

reviews.title: Way too long
reviews.text: The length is way too long even though I ordered a small. Could not use it all had to send it back after putting it on my watch and trying it on and home. Too bad to because it looks really nice.

reviews.title: Very stiff
reviews.text: The buttons don’t work when it’s inside the band. A waste of money
